delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

summary: This seminar explores themes related to the study of Intercultural Communication, Comparative Culture, and Applied Linguistics, primarily as they apply to language in use. The seminar examines differing communicative patterns and strategies between cultures. There will be frequent and ongoing negotiation of course focus, alternating between language aims (listening, spoken communication, academic writing) and content aims (IC Communication, Comparative Culture, ELT themes), depending on student needs. The seminar builds toward development of research ideas and preparation of an undergraduate thesis proposal, which should include a scholarly review of literature, method of research, and data collection leading to a final thesis written in English. Particular emphasis is placed on the use of authentic materials to examine differing aspects of communicative patterns and strategies across cultures.
aims: This seminar will explore general themes and issues related to the study of intercultural communication and comparative culture. We will look at differing aspects of communicative patterns and strategies between cultures. This course is for students planning to write their undergraduate thesis in English and the aim is to help students develop their own research ideas. A sub-theme of Seminar I is improvement of language ability in all four of the major skills with particular emphasis on spoken language (listening and speaking).
goals:
Improvement of language ability in all four of the major skills with particular emphasis on spoken language (listening and speaking).
Acquire basic skills for conducting professional research
Comparing communicative culture through critical analysis

plan.text: Cultural Resources: Authenticity and Authentic Materials in ELT
Features of transactional Speech: Commencement addresses
Features of Interactional Speech: Using Authentic Interview Data
Analysis of Authentic Dialogues: Intercultural Pragmatics (DCTs)
Analysis of Authentic Dialogues: Intercultural Pragmatics (S-H case studies)
Analysis of Authentic Communication: Intercultural Pragmatics (Email Communication)
Course wrap and final report on IC Pragmatics

prep: Students will be expected to do weekly assigned readings and prepare to lead the seminar on selected themes.
